name: Cinnamon Spiral Bread
recipeIngredient: 5 1/2 cups sifted all-purpose flour (plus more as necessary)
1 1/2 cups Quaker Oats, uncooked (quick or old-fashioned)
recipeInstructions:
1. For the dough, soften yeast in lukewarm water. Use warm water for dry yeast.
2. Pour scalded milk over sugar, salt, and shortening. Cool to lukewarm.
3. Stir in 1 cup flour. Add softened yeast and oats. Stir in enough more flour to make a soft dough.
4. Turn out the dough on a lightly floured board or canvas. Knead until smooth and satiny, about 10 minutes.
5. Shape the dough into a ball and place it in a greased bowl. Brush lightly with melted shortening. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled in size, about 1 hour.
6. Punch down the dough; cover; let rest for 10 minutes.
7. Divide the dough in half. Roll one half to form a 15 x 8-inch rectangle. Brush with melted butter and sprinkle with half of the filling made by combining sugar and cinnamon.
8. Starting with the short side, roll up as for a jelly roll.
9. Place on a greased 8-1/2" x 4-1/2" x 2-1/2" loaf pan. Brush lightly with melted butter.
10. Repeat with the other half of dough. Cover and let rise until nearly doubled in size, about 45 minutes.
11. Bake in a preheated moderate oven (375°F) for 45 to 50 minutes. Remove from pans; brush with melted butter. Cool.
